title: Photos: Highlights from the St. Patrick’s Day Parade
author: Boston.com Staff
bullets: St. Patrick's Day Parade began from Andrew Square, South Boston, with spectators lining the streets.
Participants included Scott Poce of East Bridgewater, sporting green eyebrows for the celebration.
Kathy Luginbuel and Teresa Rogers prepared to march with the Fascinators of Tampa Bay.

title: 2 men arrested after fatal overdose on Cape Cod prompts monthslong, multi-town investigation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: William Sarnie and Andre Silvia arrested for cocaine trafficking after overdose investigation on Cape Cod.
Sarnie and Silvia face felony conspiracy and drug trafficking charges in Barnstable District Court.
Investigation involved multiple local and state agencies, focusing on Nantucket and Wareham locations.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Harry Styles hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Harry Styles hosted and was the musical guest on 'Saturday Night Live' on March 14, 2026.
Styles returned as host and performer, having first done so on 'SNL' in 2019.
Ryan Gosling introduced Styles' musical performance 'Dance No More' on the episode.

title: Former Boston Public Schools teacher sentenced to 10 years in prison for child exploitation
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Magee Gavin, 35, a former Boston Public Schools teacher, was sentenced to 10 years for child exploitation.
Gavin pleaded guilty to coercion, enticement of a minor, and possession of child pornography in December 2025.
Authorities found 147 child sexual abuse material files on Gavin's phone, involving victims aged 5 to 17.

title: 5 unsigned free agents that would be possible fits for Patriots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Patriots consider Tyreek Hill for wide receiver role despite legal and injury concerns.
Marquise Brown viewed as a potential addition to enhance Patriots' receiver depth.
Linebacker Bobby Okereke could solidify Patriots' defense with his run-stopping and coverage skills.

title: Neemias Queta dominates in Celtics win over Wizards: 7 takeaways
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Neemias Queta led the Celtics with 24 points and 10 rebounds in a 111-100 win over the Wizards.
Jayson Tatum contributed 20 points and 14 rebounds but struggled with long-range shooting, making only 2 of 9 three-point attempts.
The Celtics held Wizards stars Alex Sarr and Trae Young in check, limiting them to a combined 15 points.

title: Photos: Highlights from Dropkick Murphys’ St. Patrick’s Day kickoff at MGM Music Hall
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys kicked off their St. Patrick's Day weekend with a March 13 concert at MGM Music Hall.
Openers The Vigilantes, Haywire, and Showcase Showdown supported Dropkick Murphys at the March 13 performance.
The concert featured notable moments like lead singer Ken Casey leaning into the crowd and Jeff DaRosa playing banjo.

title: ‘Feisty I think is the word’: The Boston vs. New York rivalry arrives in NWSL for Legacy FC’s debut
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC played its inaugural NWSL match against Gotham FC, drawing over 30,000 fans to Gillette Stadium.
The match ended in a 1-0 victory for Gotham FC, with a record-tying eight yellow cards issued between the teams.
Legacy FC head coach Filipa Patão highlighted her team's aggressive play, emphasizing the need to improve decision-making.

title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall at Fenway, kicking off St. Patrick’s Day celebrations with an energetic set on March 13.
The band addressed political themes, targeting the U.S. ICE and dedicating songs to the children of immigrants.
The performance included a mix of classics and new songs, with notable tracks like 'Citizen I.C.E.' and 'I’m Shipping Up to Boston'.

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett was diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11 and credits Joslin Diabetes Center for his ongoing support.
He aims to raise awareness and support for individuals living with Type 1 Diabetes through his marathon participation.
